turns.final_answer:
The neonatal mortality rate in Ethiopia is available for the years 1977 to 2024. The data represents deaths per 1,000 live births. Over this period, the rate has varied approximately between 25.23 and 67.41 deaths per 1,000 live births. The data quality is high with a full data pass check.

I have prepared a line chart to show the trend of the neonatal mortality rate in Ethiopia from 1977 to 2024.

Available breakdowns: No breakdowns available for this dataset.

args.title: Out-of-school rate for children of primary school age by Sex in Pakistan (2018)

turns.final_answer:
The out-of-school rate for children of primary school age in Pakistan for the year 2018 is as follows:
- Girls (Female): 26.7%
- Boys (Male): 19.4%

This indicates that the out-of-school rate for girls at the primary level is higher than for boys in Pakistan. The data is from the DHS 2017-18 survey and passes quality checks.

Available breakdowns: Sex

turns.question: How does it compare with boys?
turns.final_answer:
From the data for Pakistan on the out-of-school rate for children of primary school age in 2018:

- The out-of-school rate for girls is 26.7%.
- The out-of-school rate for boys is 19.4%.

This means the out-of-school rate for girls is higher than for boys by about 7.3 percentage points, indicating that girls are more likely to be out of school at the primary level compared to boys in Pakistan.

turns.question: Show me Afghanistan and Bangladesh for comparison

llm_eval.issues: Title includes Pakistan though user asked only for Afghanistan and Bangladesh; chart likely includes Pakistan data which is not requested.

args.question:
I found these indicators related to 'out-of-school rate for girls at primary level' for Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Bangladesh:
1. Out-of-school rate for children of primary school age (Pakistan 2018,

args.title: Out-of-school rate for children of primary school age by Sex in Afghanistan, Bangladesh, and Pakistan (latest available years)

turns.final_answer:
Here is the comparison of out-of-school rates for children of primary school age by sex for Afghanistan, Bangladesh, and Pakistan based on the latest available data:

- Afghanistan (2022): Girls ~60.2%, Boys ~45.8%
- Bangladesh (2019): Girls ~4.5%, Boys ~8.1%
- Pakistan (2018): Girls ~26.7%, Boys ~19.4%

Girls in Afghanistan face the highest out-of-school rate at primary level, followed by Pakistan, while Bangladesh has the lowest rates overall with boys slightly higher than girls.
